name: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Annex A
description: 12 vendor security controls mapped to ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Annex A across its four themes (Organizational, People, Physical, Technological). Suited to vendors that hold or claim ISO 27001 certification — pairs well with requesting their Statement of Applicability.

controls.title: Information security policies and roles defined
controls.description: The vendor maintains approved information security policies, reviews them at planned intervals, and assigns clear security roles and responsibilities.
controls.search_query: information security policy approved reviewed annually security roles responsibilities ISMS management commitment
controls.what_to_look_for: Evidence of a governed policy suite. Look for: information security policy, policy review cycle, ISMS, security roles and responsibilities, CISO, security committee, management approval of policies, policy acknowledgment by staff.
controls.what_good_looks_like: A.5.1 requires information security policies defined, approved by management, published, and reviewed at planned intervals. A.5.2 requires security roles and responsibilities to be defined and allocated. Best practice: an ISMS-governed policy suite reviewed at least annually with a named accountable owner.

scoring_guide.pass: Approved policy suite with review cadence and named security ownership
scoring_guide.partial: Policies mentioned but approval, review cycle, or ownership unclear
scoring_guide.fail: Evidence that no formal security policies exist
scoring_guide.no_evidence: No mention of security policies or roles

controls.title: Asset inventory and information classification
controls.description: The vendor maintains an inventory of information and associated assets, and classifies information according to sensitivity and criticality.
controls.search_query: asset inventory asset management information classification data classification sensitivity labels asset owners
controls.what_to_look_for: Evidence of asset and data governance. Look for: asset inventory, asset register, asset owners, information classification, classification levels (public/internal/confidential/restricted), labeling, handling requirements per class.
controls.what_good_looks_like: A.5.9 requires an inventory of information and other associated assets with owners. A.5.12 requires information to be classified per confidentiality, integrity, and availability needs. Best practice: a maintained asset register with owners and a 3–4 tier classification scheme applied in practice.

scoring_guide.pass: Asset inventory with owners plus a defined, applied classification scheme
scoring_guide.partial: Inventory or classification mentioned without both owners and applied tiers
scoring_guide.fail: Evidence assets/data are unmanaged or treated uniformly regardless of sensitivity
scoring_guide.no_evidence: No mention of asset inventory or classification

controls.title: Supplier and supply-chain security managed
controls.description: The vendor manages information security risks in its own supplier relationships, including ICT supply-chain risks and contractual security requirements.
controls.search_query: supplier security third-party risk management subprocessor due diligence supply chain security contractual requirements DPA
controls.what_to_look_for: Evidence of supplier risk management. Look for: supplier security policy, third-party/vendor risk assessments, subprocessor list, DPAs, contractual security clauses, supply-chain risk, periodic supplier reviews.
controls.what_good_looks_like: A.5.19 requires processes to manage security risks in supplier relationships. A.5.21 extends this to the ICT supply chain. Best practice: due diligence before onboarding suppliers, security clauses and DPAs in contracts, a published subprocessor list, and periodic reassessment of critical suppliers.

scoring_guide.pass: Supplier risk program with due diligence, contractual requirements, and ongoing review
scoring_guide.partial: Supplier management mentioned but depth or recurrence unclear
scoring_guide.fail: Evidence suppliers are engaged without security review
scoring_guide.no_evidence: No mention of supplier or supply-chain security

controls.title: Incident management planned and executed
controls.description: The vendor plans and prepares for incident management, assesses security events, and responds to confirmed incidents through a defined process.
controls.search_query: incident management plan security incident response process breach notification escalation lessons learned
controls.what_to_look_for: Evidence of incident readiness and execution. Look for: incident response plan, incident classification/severity, escalation paths, response team, breach notification commitments, post-incident review, tabletop exercises.
controls.what_good_looks_like: A.5.24 requires planning and preparing for incident management with defined processes, roles, and responsibilities. A.5.26 requires incidents to be responded to per documented procedures. Best practice: a tested plan with severity levels, defined customer notification timeframes, and post-incident reviews.

scoring_guide.pass: Documented, tested incident process with roles and notification timelines
scoring_guide.partial: Incident handling mentioned but testing, roles, or timelines unclear
scoring_guide.fail: Evidence of ad-hoc or absent incident handling

controls.title: Security awareness training delivered
controls.description: Personnel receive appropriate information security awareness education and training, with regular updates relevant to their role.
controls.search_query: security awareness training employee education phishing training annual training onboarding security program
controls.what_to_look_for: Evidence of a training program. Look for: security awareness training, annual/onboarding training, phishing simulations, role-based training, training completion tracking, security culture programs.
controls.what_good_looks_like: A.6.3 requires security awareness, education, and training with regular updates for all relevant personnel. Best practice: mandatory training at hire and at least annually, phishing simulations, and completion tracked to closure.

scoring_guide.pass: Mandatory recurring training described with tracking or reinforcement (e.g. phishing tests)
scoring_guide.partial: Training mentioned but frequency, coverage, or tracking unclear
scoring_guide.fail: Evidence that staff receive no security training
scoring_guide.no_evidence: No mention of security training or awareness

controls.title: Personnel screening and offboarding controls
controls.description: Background verification is performed before employment where legally permitted, and security responsibilities survive termination — with access and assets revoked on exit.
controls.search_query: background checks screening employment verification offboarding termination access revocation NDA confidentiality
controls.what_to_look_for: Evidence of employment lifecycle security. Look for: background checks, screening, NDAs/confidentiality agreements, termination checklist, access revoked on departure, asset return, post-employment obligations.
controls.what_good_looks_like: A.6.1 requires background verification proportional to business requirements and risk. A.6.5 requires security responsibilities that remain valid after termination to be defined and enforced. Best practice: screening for all hires, NDAs, and same-day access revocation with asset recovery on exit.

scoring_guide.pass: Screening plus formal offboarding with timely access revocation described
scoring_guide.partial: One of screening/offboarding described, or timeliness unclear
scoring_guide.fail: Evidence departed staff retain access or no screening occurs
scoring_guide.no_evidence: No mention of screening or offboarding

controls.title: Physical security perimeters and entry control
controls.description: Facilities processing sensitive information are protected by defined physical perimeters and appropriate entry controls (badges, visitor management, monitored access).
controls.search_query: physical security data center access badge entry controls visitor management CCTV perimeter security facilities
controls.what_to_look_for: Evidence of physical protections. Look for: physical security perimeters, badge/card access, biometric entry, visitor logs and escorts, CCTV, 24/7 security staff, data-center certifications (SOC 2/ISO of the facility), locked server rooms.
controls.what_good_looks_like: A.7.1 requires security perimeters protecting areas with sensitive information. A.7.2 requires those areas to have appropriate entry controls. Best practice: layered physical controls; for cloud-hosted vendors, evidence that hosting providers' certified data centers (e.g. AWS/GCP/Azure) provide this layer counts.

scoring_guide.pass: Physical/entry controls described directly or inherited via certified hosting providers
scoring_guide.partial: Physical security mentioned generally without specific controls
scoring_guide.fail: Evidence of unrestricted physical access to systems

controls.title: Privileged access and strong authentication
controls.description: Privileged access rights are restricted and managed, and secure authentication — including multi-factor authentication — controls access to systems.
controls.search_query: privileged access management multi-factor authentication MFA secure authentication admin accounts least privilege
controls.what_to_look_for: Evidence of access rigor. Look for: MFA/2FA, privileged access management, separate admin accounts, just-in-time elevation, least privilege, SSO with strong auth, session logging for admins.
controls.what_good_looks_like: A.8.2 requires allocation and management of privileged access rights to be restricted. A.8.5 requires secure authentication per the access control policy. Best practice: MFA for all users (phishing-resistant preferred), tightly managed and logged privileged accounts.

scoring_guide.pass: MFA for all users plus managed privileged access described
scoring_guide.partial: MFA or PAM described but scope/enforcement unclear or admin-only
scoring_guide.fail: Evidence of password-only auth or unmanaged admin accounts
scoring_guide.no_evidence: No mention of authentication or privileged access

controls.title: Cryptography applied to data at rest and in transit
controls.description: Rules for effective use of cryptography, including key management, are defined and implemented — encrypting sensitive data at rest and in transit.
controls.search_query: encryption cryptography AES-256 TLS key management KMS data at rest in transit crypto policy
controls.what_to_look_for: Evidence of applied cryptography. Look for: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.2+/1.3 in transit, key management (KMS/HSM), key rotation, crypto policy, encrypted backups.
controls.what_good_looks_like: A.8.24 requires rules for effective use of cryptography including key management. Best practice: AES-256 at rest (including backups), TLS 1.2+ everywhere, keys managed in a KMS/HSM separate from data with rotation.

scoring_guide.pass: Encryption at rest and in transit with key management described
scoring_guide.partial: Encryption described but scope or key management unclear

controls.title: Technical vulnerabilities managed
controls.description: Information about technical vulnerabilities is obtained, exposure is evaluated, and appropriate measures (scanning, patching, testing) are taken in a timely way.
controls.search_query: vulnerability management scanning patching penetration testing remediation SLA CVE patch cadence
controls.what_to_look_for: Evidence of vulnerability lifecycle. Look for: vulnerability scanning cadence, patch SLAs by severity, penetration testing, bug bounty/disclosure program, remediation tracking.
controls.what_good_looks_like: A.8.8 requires obtaining vulnerability information, evaluating exposure, and taking appropriate measures. Best practice: automated scanning at least monthly, severity-based patch SLAs (criticals in days), annual independent penetration tests, findings tracked to closure.

scoring_guide.pass: Scanning + patch SLAs + independent testing described with cadence
scoring_guide.partial: Some vulnerability practices described without cadence or SLAs
scoring_guide.fail: Evidence of known vulnerabilities left unmanaged

controls.title: Logging and monitoring in place
controls.description: Logs of activities, exceptions, and events are produced, protected, and analyzed; networks and systems are monitored for anomalous behavior.
controls.search_query: logging monitoring SIEM audit logs anomaly detection alerting log retention centralized logs
controls.what_to_look_for: Evidence of observability for security. Look for: centralized logging, SIEM, log retention periods, log protection/immutability, monitoring/alerting, anomaly detection, SOC coverage.
controls.what_good_looks_like: A.8.15 requires logs to be produced, stored, protected, and analyzed. A.8.16 requires monitoring for anomalous behavior with appropriate action. Best practice: centralized, protected logs retained 12+ months with automated alerting and defined response.

scoring_guide.pass: Centralized, protected logging plus active monitoring/alerting described
scoring_guide.partial: Logging or monitoring described but centralization, protection, or retention unclear
scoring_guide.fail: Evidence of absent or unprotected logging
scoring_guide.no_evidence: No mention of logging or monitoring

controls.title: Backups and redundancy for availability
controls.description: Backups of information, software, and systems are maintained and regularly tested, and processing facilities have redundancy sufficient to meet availability requirements.
controls.search_query: backup restore testing redundancy failover disaster recovery RTO RPO availability multi-region
controls.what_to_look_for: Evidence of recoverability. Look for: backup frequency and encryption, restore testing, DR plans and exercises, RTO/RPO targets, redundancy/failover, multi-AZ or multi-region architecture, uptime SLAs.
controls.what_good_looks_like: A.8.13 requires backups maintained and tested per policy. A.8.14 requires redundancy sufficient for availability requirements. Best practice: automated encrypted backups with periodic restore tests, defined RTO/RPO, and redundant infrastructure with a published SLA.

scoring_guide.pass: Tested backups plus redundancy/DR with recovery targets described
scoring_guide.partial: Backups or redundancy described without testing or targets
scoring_guide.fail: Evidence that backups or redundancy are absent
scoring_guide.no_evidence: No mention of backups or availability measures
